title: Oondreswarar Temple
tagline: Oondreswarar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Oondreswarar Temple (ஊன்றீஸ்வரர் கோயில் ) (also called Poondi Temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Poondi, a village in Tiruvallur district in the Sou
overview.description: Oondreswarar Temple (ஊன்றீஸ்வரர் கோயில் ) (also called Poondi Temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Poondi, a village in Tiruvallur district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshiped as Oondreswarar, and is represented by the lingam. His consort Parvati is depicted as Minnoli Amman. The temple is located on the Northern banks of Poondi reservoir on the Thiruvallur - Thiruthani road, 11 km (6.8 mi) away from the town.

topPlaces.name: Attirampakkam
topPlaces.description: Attirampakkam, or Athirampakkam is a village near river Kortallaiyar located 60 kilometers away from Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India. The oldest stone tools in India were discovered near the village, which became the type site for the Madrasian culture.

topPlaces.name: Poondi reservoir
topPlaces.description: Sathyamoorthy Saagar, also known as Poondy Reservoir, is the reservoir constructed across Kotralai (alias) Kosasthalai River in Tiruvallur district of Tamil Nadu state in India. It acts as the important drinking water source for Chennai Municipal Corporation which is located 60 km away from the lake.

topPlaces.name: Gudiyam Cave
topPlaces.description: Gudiyam Caves are rock shelters in South India and known for prehistoric stone tools and culture. They were first identified by British geologist Robert Bruce Foote.

topPlaces.name: Kaivandur
topPlaces.description: Kaivandur is a village in Tiruvallur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located between the town of Thiruvallur and the Thiruthani temple.

topPlaces.name: Ikkadu Kandigai
topPlaces.description: Ikkadu Kandigai is a village near Tiruvallur town in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is five kilometers from Tiruvallur town.

topPlaces.name: Vaseeswarar Temple
topPlaces.description: Vaseeswarar Temple (also called Pasoornathar Temple, Thirupasoor temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Thirupasoor, a village in Tiruvallur district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshiped as Vaseeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.

items.note: Attirampakkam, or Athirampakkam is a village near river Kortallaiyar located 60 kilometers away from Chennai, Tamil Nadu, India. The oldest stone tools in India were discovered near the village, which became the type site for the Madrasian culture.

Sathyamoorthy Saagar, also known as Poondy Reservoir, is the reservoir constructed across Kotralai (alias) Kosasthalai River in Tiruvallur district of Tamil Nadu state in India. It acts as the important drinking water source for Chennai Municipal Corporation which is located 60 km away from the lake.
Gudiyam Caves are rock shelters in South India and known for prehistoric stone tools and culture. They were first identified by British geologist Robert Bruce Foote.
Kaivandur is a village in Tiruvallur district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located between the town of Thiruvallur and the Thiruthani temple.
Ikkadu Kandigai is a village near Tiruvallur town in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is five kilometers from Tiruvallur town.

Vaseeswarar Temple (also called Pasoornathar Temple, Thirupasoor temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Thirupasoor, a village in Tiruvallur district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshiped as Vaseeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Oondreswarar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Oondreswarar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Oondreswarar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (45 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Oondreswarar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Oondreswarar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Oondreswarar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Oondreswarar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Oondreswarar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Attirampakkam, Poondi reservoir, Gudiyam Cave, Kaivandur, Ikkadu Kandigai.
